title: Cell-free lysate (E. coli) preparation with sonication
author: Nadanai Laohakunakorn
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8nthven
materials: Prepare all materials for bacterial culture This protocol will make 200 mL of culture to yield around 1 mL of lysate with a protein concentration of around 40 - 80 mg/mL.
Preparation of 1M stock solutions of tris acetate, magnesium glutamate, and potassium glutamate
Finally, carefully discard as much supernatant as possible.

steps.title: Reconstitute LB media from premix as per instructions on box
steps.instruction: Reconstitute LB media from premix as per instructions on box. Protocol has also been successfully carried out with 2x YTP and 2x YTPG (34°C, original). Cells grow slower on YTP media (~5-6h to OD 1.5-1.8) compared to ~4h in LB.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of LB medium...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of LB medium to a culture tube and inoculate with small amount of bacteria from glycerol stock. Strains used successfully with this protocol are BL21 (DE3) and BL21 star (DE3) (lacking RNaseE), T7 and E. coli RNAP Rosetta (DE3) for oscillators (contains rare tRNA encoding plasmid, good for eukaryotic protein expression) Top10, Top10-GamS (GamS inhibits RecBCD degradation of linear DNA), E. coli RNAP, alpha-complementation M15 (E. coli RNAP, alpha-complementation) DH5alpha (E. coli RNAP, alpha-complementation)

steps.title: Grow overnight culture in an incubator...
steps.instruction: Grow overnight culture in an incubator at 37 °C and 200 RPM. Make sure cap is in loose position to allow for air exchange

steps.title: Autoclave LB media for 00:20:00 at...
steps.instruction: Autoclave LB media for 00:20:00 at 121 °C along with tips and dishes

steps.title: Add 1 mL of overnight culture...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of overnight culture to 200 mL of LB medium in a 500 mL Erlenmeyer flask.

steps.title: Measure and record OD600 of overnight...
steps.instruction: Measure and record OD600 of overnight culture at 10x dilution : pipette 900 µL LB and 100 µL overnight culture into a cuvette and take absorbance readings at 600 nm. OD600 should be around 4 (0.4 at 10x dilution)

steps.title: Incubate the culture for 04:00:00 ,...
steps.instruction: Incubate the culture for 04:00:00 , 37 °C , and 200 RPM. At the end of the step, the OD600 should be ~1.5-2. While Kwon and Jewett grow to OD600 = 3, we grow instead for a fixed time. They observe varying robustness of yield to final OD which is strain dependent. In the end we should probably grow to fixed OD. Activity of cells harvested at different densities, grown in 1L 2xYTPG and sonicated at 1.5 mL/556 J. From Kwon and Jewett 2015, Figure 3, CC-BY license.

steps.title: Put 50 mL of culture into...
steps.instruction: Put 50 mL of culture into each of four 50 mL Falcon tubes (one of which has been labelled), and spin at 4000 rpm, = 3220g 4 °C 00:20:00 . Tubes in swing-bucket rotor A-4-62.

steps.title: Put tubes back On ice ;...
steps.instruction: Put tubes back On ice ; keep bacterial pellet cold as much as possible.

steps.title: A few minutes before incubation step...
steps.instruction: A few minutes before incubation step finishes, prepare centrifuge and tubes

steps.title: Cool down big centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R)...
steps.instruction: Cool down big centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R) to 4 °C with fast temp mode; this takes around 10 mins.

steps.title: Weigh one 50 mL Falcon tube...
steps.instruction: Weigh one 50 mL Falcon tube along with its cap, and record. Clearly label the tube as well as its cap; this tube will be used to spin the final pellet.

steps.title: As soon as the incubation finishes,...
steps.instruction: As soon as the incubation finishes, put the Erlenmeyer flask On ice to arrest growth.

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant using a pipette,...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant using a pipette, then add 10 mL of Buffer A (no DTT) to each pellet. Resuspend by carefully pipetting up and down. Finally, transfer all four parts into the single labelled tube.

steps.title: Balance centrifuge with a second tube containing 40 mL water
steps.instruction: Balance centrifuge with a second tube containing 40 mL water. Then spin at 4000 rpm, = 3220g 4 °C 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant using a pipette,...
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant using a pipette, then add 10 mL of Buffer A to the pellet. Then spin at 4000 rpm, = 3220 g 4 °C 00:10:00 .

steps.title: go to step #10 and repeat spin once more
steps.instruction: go to step #10 and repeat spin once more.

steps.title: Weigh the tube containing the pellet...
steps.instruction: Weigh the tube containing the pellet (make sure cap is on), and record: %s %s The wet mass of cells is the difference between this weight and the dry weight measured earlier. This is typically around ~1 g.

steps.title: Flash freeze the cells using liquid...
steps.instruction: Flash freeze the cells using liquid nitrogen, respecting all safety procedures (wear protective glasses as there is danger of tube explosion etc.). Store the cells at -80 °C . Flash-freezing is optional (the protocol will work without) but it serves two purposes: Convenient pause point Frozen cells, once rethawed, lyse slightly more easily than fresh cells. So this step must be kept consistent to ensure reproducible protocol.

steps.title: Keeping the pellet On ice ,...
steps.instruction: Keeping the pellet On ice , add Buffer A with DTT : 1 mL buffer per 1 g wet mass as determined earlier. This ratio is critical . The DTT must be prepared fresh as it will degrade otherwise.

steps.title: Resuspend by vortexing, and put back On ice
steps.instruction: Resuspend by vortexing, and put back On ice

steps.title: Put 1 mL exactly of resuspended...
steps.instruction: Put 1 mL exactly of resuspended cells into a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube. This volume is critical. The larger the volume the more robust the lysis: Yield as a function of volume and sonication energy. From Kwon and Jewett Figure 4, CC-BY license. So if possible, scale up the production to achieve (ideally) at least 5 mL lysis volumes ( = 5 g wet mass).

steps.title: Place the tube in an ice...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in an ice bath - ice mixed with water - and place the sonicator tip inside the tube so that it is immersed as much as possible but no part of the tip touches the tube surface . This is one of the most variable parts of the protocol. A suggested technique is to drop the tip to the bottom of the tube, and then raise it slightly. Holding the tube using a cool rack (e.g. Fisher 15592801) is recommended.

steps.title: Sonicate until 400J has been achieved,...
steps.instruction: Sonicate until 400J has been achieved, using 50% amplitude and pulses of 10s : 10s (i.e. energy for 10s followed by pause for 10s). This typically takes around 00:01:24 but the time is variable. The total sonication energy is a critical parameter.

steps.title: Cool down the small centrifuge (Eppendorf...
steps.instruction: Cool down the small centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424R) to 4 °C using the fast temp mode: this takes around 20 minutes.

steps.title: Balance and centrifuge the lysate at...
steps.instruction: Balance and centrifuge the lysate at 12000 x g, = 11304 rpm 4 °C 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. To prevent any transfer of bacterial debris, it is critical that you do not take all the lysate .

steps.title: Place the lysate in an incubator...
steps.instruction: Place the lysate in an incubator at 37 °C , 200 RPM for 01:30:00 , to degrade remaining DNA and RNA. This is called the run-off reaction. Even though Kwon and Jewett observe deterioriation in BL21 production yield with run-off, we do not see the same behaviour. If E. coli RNAP will be used, do dialysis as well as run-off.

steps.title: Making sure the centrifuge is still...
steps.instruction: Making sure the centrifuge is still cool, centrifuge the lysate at 12000 x g, = 11304 rpm 4 °C 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Again, do not take all the lysate . Aliquot around 3 µL into separate tube for a Bradford assay.

steps.title: Keeping everything On ice , aliquot...
steps.instruction: Keeping everything On ice , aliquot the lysate into small tubes as required (around 25 µL per tube is recommended).

steps.title: Flash-freeze the remaining tubes in liquid...
steps.instruction: Flash-freeze the remaining tubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C

steps.title: To perform the Bradford assay, dilute...
steps.instruction: To perform the Bradford assay, dilute 1 µL of the lysate in 99 µL of Buffer A. Mix 5 µL of this diluted lysate with 250 µL of Bradford reagent. Incubate for 00:05:00 before measuring the absorbance using a Nanodrop. The final protein concentration is typically 40 - 80 mg/mL.

steps.title: Weigh 12
steps.instruction: Weigh 12.11 g of tris base (121.14 g/mol), add it to 100 mL of deionized water, and adjust to pH 8.2 with acetic acid, to make 1M tris acetate stock.

steps.title: Weigh 38
steps.instruction: Weigh 38.86 g of magnesium glutamate (388.61 g/mol), and add it to 100 mL of deionized water, to make 1M magnesium glutamate stock.

steps.title: Weigh 20
steps.instruction: Weigh 20.32 g of potassium glutamate (203.23 g/mol), and add it to 100 mL of deionized water, to make 1M potassium glutamate stock.

steps.title: Add 10 mL of 1M tris acetate pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of 1M tris acetate pH 8.2, 14 mL of 1M magnesium glutamate, and 60 mL of 1M potassium glutamate, and fill to 1 L with deionized water to make buffer A (10 mM tris acetate, 14 mM magnesium glutamate, 60 mM potassium glutamate).

steps.title: Store at
steps.instruction: Store at

steps.title: Add 2 µL of 1M DTT...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of 1M DTT to 1000 µL Buffer A (final concentration 2 mM) for the lysis solution, or 1.8 mL of 1M DTT to 900 mL of Buffer A for the dialysis solution. This should be made fresh every time but if short-term storage is required, put at -20 °C

steps.title: (If required) induce after 2 hours with 0
steps.instruction: (If required) induce after 2 hours with 0.4 mM IPTG ( 800 µL, of 100 mM stock in 200 mL culture) to express T7 polymerase in the BL21 (DE3) strains.

steps.title: Rehydrate the required number of 10k...
steps.instruction: Rehydrate the required number of 10k MCWO cassettes in Buffer A+DTT in a beaker at 4 °C for 00:02:00

steps.title: Load cassettes with 2
steps.instruction: Load cassettes with 2.5 mL, max (partial loading possible) of extract and dialyze for 03:00:00 f 4 °C with stirring.

steps.title: Finally load extract into centrifuge and...
steps.instruction: Finally load extract into centrifuge and centrifuge the lysate at 12000 x g, = 11304 rpm 4 °C 00:10:00

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Again, do not take all the lysate. Proceed to step 27.

steps.title: Prepare Buffer A + DTT and...
steps.instruction: Prepare Buffer A + DTT and add 900 mL to a 1 L beaker at 4 °C . Add a magnetic stir bar.
